Setup and Software Environment

add snippet here

Load Packages

Show code 
library(tidyverse)  # data manipulation + plotting
library(psych)      # scale construction + reliability
library(afex)       # ANOVA models + effect sizes
library(emmeans)    # estimated marginal means + confidence intervals

Data Source and Analysis Dataset

add snippet here

Import Dataset

Show code 
study_raw <- read_csv("data/relationship_preferences_data.csv")

Create Analysis Dataset

Show code 
study_vars <- c(
  
      "condition",  
      "age", "gender", "relationship",
  
      "IAS_1","IAS_2","IAS_3","IAS_4", "IAS_5","IAS_6",
      "IAS_7","IAS_8","IAS_9","IAS_10", 
      
      "AAS_1","AAS_2","AAS_3","AAS_4","AAS_5","AAS_6","AAS_7",
      "AAS_8","AAS_9","AAS_10","AAS_11","AAS_12", "AAS_13","AAS_14",
      "AAS_15","AAS_16","AAS_17","AAS_18",  
      
      "ETTR_1", "ETTR_2", "ETTR_3", "ETTR_4", "ETTR_5", "ETTR_6", 
      "ETTR_7", "ETTR_8", "ETTR_9", "ETTR_10", "ETTR_11", "ETTR_12", 
      "ETTR_13", "ETTR_14", "ETTR_15", "ETTR_16", "ETTR_17", "ETTR_18", 
      "ETTR_19"
)


study_data <- study_raw %>%
  select(all_of(study_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

Variable Preparation

add snippet text here

Prepare Participant ID and Experimental Condition

Show code 
study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    id = row_number(),
    condition = factor(
      condition,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Low", "High")
    )
  )

Reverse-Code Scale Items

add snippet here about reverse coding the

Show code 
study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    IAS_3r = 8 - IAS_3,
    IAS_4r = 8 - IAS_4,
    IAS_5r = 8 - IAS_5,
    
    ETTR_2r  = 7 - ETTR_2,
    ETTR_5r  = 7 - ETTR_5,
    ETTR_7r  = 7 - ETTR_7,
    ETTR_10r = 7 - ETTR_10,
    ETTR_13r = 7 - ETTR_13,
    ETTR_16r = 7 - ETTR_16,
    ETTR_18r = 7 - ETTR_18,
    
    AAS_8r  = 6 - AAS_8,
    AAS_13r = 6 - AAS_13,
    AAS_17r = 6 - AAS_17,
    AAS_2r  = 6 - AAS_2,
    AAS_16r = 6 - AAS_16,
    AAS_18r = 6 - AAS_18,
    
    AAS_3r  = 6 - AAS_3,
    AAS_4r  = 6 - AAS_4,
    AAS_9r  = 6 - AAS_9,
    AAS_10r = 6 - AAS_10,
    AAS_11r = 6 - AAS_11,
    AAS_15r = 6 - AAS_15
  )

Check Reverse Coding

Show code 
study_data %>%
  select(IAS_3,IAS_3r, ETTR_2, ETTR_2r, AAS_3, AAS_3r) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  knitr::kable()
IAS_3 IAS_3r ETTR_2 ETTR_2r AAS_3 AAS_3r
4 4 3 4 1 5
2 6 2 5 3 3
4 4 2 5 2 4
3 5 3 4 1 5
3 5 4 3 1 5
6 2 3 4 5 1
2 6 5 2 3 3
4 4 4 3 2 4
7 1 2 5 3 3
4 4 4 3 3 3

add snippet here about output above

Create Scale Scores

add snippet here

Note: For this analysis workflow, the AAS items were reverse-scored so that all attachment scale scores point in a positive direction. Higher scores indicate more adaptive attachment-related outcomes. Specifically, higher total AAS scores indicate higher overall attachment security, higher closeness scores indicate greater comfort with closeness, higher dependence scores indicate greater comfort depending on others, and higher anxiety-security scores indicate lower attachment anxiety.

Show code 
# Define IAS social attraction items
IAS_items <- c("IAS_1", "IAS_2", "IAS_3r", "IAS_4r", "IAS_5r", "IAS_6")

# Define revised AAS close, depend, and anxiety-security items
aas_close_items <- c("AAS_1", "AAS_6", "AAS_8r", "AAS_12", "AAS_13r", "AAS_17r")

aas_depend_items <- c("AAS_2r", "AAS_5", "AAS_7", "AAS_14", "AAS_16r", "AAS_18r")

aas_anxiety_items <- c("AAS_3r", "AAS_4r", "AAS_9r", "AAS_10r", "AAS_11r", "AAS_15r")

aas_total_items <- c(aas_close_items, aas_depend_items, aas_anxiety_items)

# Define ETRR relationship expectation items
ETTR_items <- c("ETTR_1", "ETTR_2r", "ETTR_3", "ETTR_4", "ETTR_5r", "ETTR_6", 
                "ETTR_7r", "ETTR_8", "ETTR_9", "ETTR_10r", "ETTR_11", "ETTR_12", 
                "ETTR_13r", "ETTR_14", "ETTR_15", "ETTR_16r", "ETTR_17", "ETTR_18r", "ETTR_19")

study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    IAS_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(IAS_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_close_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_close_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_depend_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_depend_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_anxiety_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_anxiety_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_total_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_total_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    ETTR_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(ETTR_items)), na.rm = TRUE)
  )

Check Scale Scores

Show code 
study_data %>%
  select(IAS_scale, aas_close_scale, aas_depend_scale, aas_anxiety_scale, aas_total_scale, ETTR_scale) %>%
  summary()
   IAS_scale     aas_close_scale aas_depend_scale aas_anxiety_scale
 Min.   :1.667   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.167    Min.   :1.000    
 1st Qu.:4.833   1st Qu.:2.833   1st Qu.:2.333    1st Qu.:2.367    
 Median :5.333   Median :3.333   Median :2.833    Median :3.167    
 Mean   :5.243   Mean   :3.361   Mean   :2.845    Mean   :3.166    
 3rd Qu.:6.000   3rd Qu.:3.833   3rd Qu.:3.417    3rd Qu.:3.833    
 Max.   :7.000   Max.   :5.000   Max.   :4.833    Max.   :5.000    
 NA's   :1                                                         
 aas_total_scale   ETTR_scale   
 Min.   :1.333   Min.   :1.789  
 1st Qu.:2.587   1st Qu.:3.053  
 Median :3.111   Median :4.053  
 Mean   :3.124   Mean   :3.882  
 3rd Qu.:3.611   3rd Qu.:4.632  
 Max.   :4.611   Max.   :5.895
Show code 
study_data %>%
  summarise(
    IAS_missing = mean(is.na(IAS_scale)),
    aas_close_missing = mean(is.na(aas_close_scale)),
    aas_depend_missing = mean(is.na(aas_depend_scale)),
    aas_anxiety_missing = mean(is.na(aas_anxiety_scale)),
    aas_total_missing = mean(is.na(aas_total_scale)),
    ETTR_missing = mean(is.na(ETTR_scale))
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable()
IAS_missing aas_close_missing aas_depend_missing aas_anxiety_missing aas_total_missing ETTR_missing
0.0105263 0 0 0 0 0

add snippet here about output above

Create Attachment Anxiety Groups

add snippet here

Show code 
# Create median values for the attachment scale scores
aas_total_median <- median(study_data$aas_total_scale, na.rm = TRUE)
aas_close_median <- median(study_data$aas_close_scale, na.rm = TRUE)
aas_depend_median <- median(study_data$aas_depend_scale, na.rm = TRUE)
aas_anxiety_median <- median(study_data$aas_anxiety_scale, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create median-split attachment groups
study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    aas_total_group = if_else(aas_total_scale <= aas_total_median, "Lower attachment security", "Higher attachment security"),
    aas_total_group = factor(aas_total_group, levels = c("Lower attachment security", "Higher attachment security")),
    
    aas_close_group = if_else(aas_close_scale <= aas_close_median, "Lower closeness security", "Higher closeness security"),
    aas_close_group = factor(aas_close_group, levels = c("Lower closeness security", "Higher closeness security")),
    
    aas_depend_group = if_else(aas_depend_scale <= aas_depend_median, "Lower dependence security", "Higher dependence security"),
    aas_depend_group = factor(aas_depend_group, levels = c("Lower dependence security", "Higher dependence security")),
    
    aas_anxiety_group = if_else(aas_anxiety_scale <= aas_anxiety_median, "Lower anxiety security", "Higher anxiety security"),
    aas_anxiety_group = factor(aas_anxiety_group, levels = c("Lower anxiety security", "Higher anxiety security"))
  )

# Check final attachment group sizes
study_data %>%
  count(aas_total_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()
aas_total_group n
Lower attachment security 49
Higher attachment security 46
Show code 
study_data %>%
  count(aas_close_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()
aas_close_group n
Lower closeness security 48
Higher closeness security 47
Show code 
study_data %>%
  count(aas_depend_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()
aas_depend_group n
Lower dependence security 50
Higher dependence security 45
Show code 
study_data %>%
  count(aas_anxiety_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()
aas_anxiety_group n
Lower anxiety security 51
Higher anxiety security 44

add snippet here about output above

Check Scale Reliability

add snippet here

Show code 
# Create item-only data frames for each scale
IAS_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(IAS_items))
aas_close_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_close_items))
aas_depend_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_depend_items))
aas_anxiety_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_anxiety_items))
aas_total_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_total_items))
ETTR_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(ETTR_items))

# Compute internal consistency estimates
IAS_alpha <- psych::alpha(IAS_item_df)
IAS_omega <- psych::omega(IAS_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_close_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_close_item_df)
aas_close_omega <- psych::omega(aas_close_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_depend_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_depend_item_df)
aas_depend_omega <- psych::omega(aas_depend_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_anxiety_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_anxiety_item_df)
aas_anxiety_omega <- psych::omega(aas_anxiety_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_total_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_total_item_df)
aas_total_omega <- psych::omega(aas_total_item_df, plot = FALSE)

ETTR_alpha <- psych::alpha(ETTR_item_df)
ETTR_omega <- psych::omega(ETTR_item_df, plot = FALSE)

# Print only alpha and omega coefficients
tibble(
  scale = c("Social Attraction", "AAS Close Security", "AAS Depend Security", "AAS Anxiety Security", "AAS Total Security", "Relationship Expectation"),
  alpha = c(IAS_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_close_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_depend_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_anxiety_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_total_alpha$total$raw_alpha, ETTR_alpha$total$raw_alpha),
  omega = c(IAS_omega$omega.tot, aas_close_omega$omega.tot, aas_depend_omega$omega.tot, aas_anxiety_omega$omega.tot, aas_total_omega$omega.tot, ETTR_omega$omega.tot)
) %>%
  knitr::kable()
scale alpha omega
Social Attraction 0.8968806 0.9514521
AAS Close Security 0.7371310 0.8174746
AAS Depend Security 0.7550210 0.8630135
AAS Anxiety Security 0.8796060 0.9251593
AAS Total Security 0.8733301 0.9158943
Relationship Expectation 0.9643066 0.9726005

Correlations Among the DVs

add snippet here

Show code 
# Correlations for the main scale scores
psych::corr.test(
  study_data %>% 
    select(
      IAS_scale,
      aas_close_scale,
      aas_depend_scale,
      aas_anxiety_scale,
      aas_total_scale,
      ETTR_scale
    )
)
Call:psych::corr.test(x = study_data %>% select(IAS_scale, aas_close_scale, 
    aas_depend_scale, aas_anxiety_scale, aas_total_scale, ETTR_scale))

Primary Analyses

Add snippet about what ANOVAS you are going to conduct.

Relationship Expectation Model 1: Total Attachment Security

Show code 
# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting relationship expectation scores
# DV: ETTR_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_total_group
a1_ETTR_total <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_total_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_ETTR_total
Anova Table (Type 3 tests)

Response: ETTR_scale
                     Effect    df  MSE         F  ges p.value
1                 condition 1, 91 0.75 32.97 *** .266   <.001
2           aas_total_group 1, 91 0.75      1.49 .016    .225
3 condition:aas_total_group 1, 91 0.75      0.17 .002    .680
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '+' 0.1 ' ' 1

Add snippet about output here.

Estimated Means for Relationship Expectation: Total Attachment Security

Show code 
emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ condition)
 condition emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Low         3.35 0.129 91     3.09     3.61
 High        4.38 0.123 91     4.13     4.62

Results are averaged over the levels of: aas_total_group 
Confidence level used: 0.95
Show code 
emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ aas_total_group)
 aas_total_group            emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Lower attachment security    3.75 0.125 91     3.50     4.00
 Higher attachment security   3.97 0.128 91     3.72     4.23

Results are averaged over the levels of: condition 
Confidence level used: 0.95
Show code 
emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ condition * aas_total_group)
 condition aas_total_group            emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Low       Lower attachment security    3.28 0.189 91     2.90     3.65
 High      Lower attachment security    4.23 0.164 91     3.90     4.55
 Low       Higher attachment security   3.42 0.177 91     3.07     3.77
 High      Higher attachment security   4.52 0.185 91     4.15     4.89

Confidence level used: 0.95

Add snippet about estimated means here.

Relationship Expectation Model 2: Closeness Security

Show code 
# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting relationship expectation scores
# DV: ETTR_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_close_group
a1_ETTR_close <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_close_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_ETTR_close
Anova Table (Type 3 tests)

Response: ETTR_scale
                     Effect    df  MSE         F   ges p.value
1                 condition 1, 91 0.75 33.37 ***  .268   <.001
2           aas_close_group 1, 91 0.75      2.00  .021    .161
3 condition:aas_close_group 1, 91 0.75      0.03 <.001    .853
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '+' 0.1 ' ' 1

# DV: ETTR_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_depend_group

  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",

)

a1_ETTR_depend
Anova Table (Type 3 tests)

Response: ETTR_scale
                      Effect    df  MSE         F  ges p.value
1                  condition 1, 91 0.74 32.98 *** .266   <.001
2           aas_depend_group 1, 91 0.74      1.74 .019    .190
3 condition:aas_depend_group 1, 91 0.74      0.75 .008    .388

  dv = "ETTR_scale",

)

a1_ETTR_anxiety
Anova Table (Type 3 tests)

Response: ETTR_scale
                       Effect    df  MSE         F   ges p.value

2           aas_anxiety_group 1, 91 0.75      1.90  .020    .172
3 condition:aas_anxiety_group 1, 91 0.75      0.05 <.001    .824

Social Attraction Model 1: Total Attachment Security

Show code 
# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting social attraction scores
# DV: IAS_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_total_group
a1_IAS_total <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "IAS_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_total_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_IAS_total
Anova Table (Type 3 tests)

Response: IAS_scale
                     Effect    df  MSE         F  ges p.value
1                 condition 1, 90 1.01 16.51 *** .155   <.001
2           aas_total_group 1, 90 1.01   7.48 ** .077    .008
3 condition:aas_total_group 1, 90 1.01      0.36 .004    .551
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '+' 0.1 ' ' 1

Add snippet about output here.

Estimated Means for Social Attraction: Total Attachment Security

Show code 
emmeans(a1_IAS_total, ~ condition)
 condition emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Low         4.79 0.152 90     4.49     5.09
 High        5.64 0.143 90     5.36     5.92

Results are averaged over the levels of: aas_total_group 
Confidence level used: 0.95
Show code 
emmeans(a1_IAS_total, ~ aas_total_group)
 aas_total_group            emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Lower attachment security    4.93 0.147 90     4.64     5.22
 Higher attachment security   5.50 0.148 90     5.21     5.80

Results are averaged over the levels of: condition 
Confidence level used: 0.95
Show code 
emmeans(a1_IAS_total, ~ condition * aas_total_group)
 condition aas_total_group            emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 Low       Lower attachment security    4.44 0.225 90     3.99     4.89
 High      Lower attachment security    5.42 0.190 90     5.04     5.79
 Low       Higher attachment security   5.14 0.205 90     4.73     5.55
 High      Higher attachment security   5.86 0.215 90     5.44     6.29

Confidence level used: 0.95

Add snippet about estimated means here.

                     Effect    df  MSE         F   ges p.value

Add snippet about estimated means here.

Figures

Add snippet here.

Relationship Expectation Interaction Plot: Total Attachment Security

Show code 
p_ETTR_total_bar <- ggplot(
  as.data.frame(emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ condition * aas_total_group)),
  aes(
    x = condition,
    y = emmean,
    fill = aas_total_group
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.55,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Communication Condition",
    y = "Mean Relationship Expectation Score",
    fill = "Total Attachment Security"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Lower attachment security" = "#E76F51",  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
      "Higher attachment security" = "#2A9D8F"  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:6
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(1, 6)
  ) +
  theme_classic(
    base_size = 14
  )

p_ETTR_total_bar

Add snippet about the above figure here.

Relationship Expectation Interaction Plot: Closeness Security

Show code 
p_ETTR_close_bar <- ggplot(
  as.data.frame(emmeans(a1_ETTR_close, ~ condition * aas_close_group)),
  aes(
    x = condition,
    y = emmean,
    fill = aas_close_group
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.55,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL

    width = 0.12,

      "Higher closeness security" = "#2A9D8F"  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:6
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(1, 6)
  ) +
  theme_classic(
    base_size = 14
  )

    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Communication Condition",
    y = "Mean Social Attraction Score",
    fill = "Dependence Security"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Lower dependence security" = "#E76F51",  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
      "Higher dependence security" = "#2A9D8F"  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(1, 7)
  ) +
  theme_classic(
    base_size = 14
  )

p_IAS_depend_bar

Add snippet about the above figure here.

Social Attraction Interaction Plot: Anxiety Security

Show code 
p_IAS_anxiety_bar <- ggplot(
  as.data.frame(emmeans(a1_IAS_anxiety, ~ condition * aas_anxiety_group)),
  aes(
    x = condition,
    y = emmean,
    fill = aas_anxiety_group
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.55,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Communication Condition",
    y = "Mean Social Attraction Score",
    fill = "Anxiety Security"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Lower anxiety security" = "#E76F51",  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
      "Higher anxiety security" = "#2A9D8F"  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(1, 7)
  ) +
  theme_classic(
    base_size = 14
  )

## About This Report

*add information here*

## Study Overview

*add study information here (like introduction)*

*dont change this chunk*

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Setup and Software Environment

*add snippet here*

**Load Packages**

```{r}
#| label: load_packages
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)  # data manipulation + plotting
library(psych)      # scale construction + reliability
library(afex)       # ANOVA models + effect sizes
library(emmeans)    # estimated marginal means + confidence intervals
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Data Source and Analysis Dataset

*add snippet here*

### Import Dataset

```{r}
#| label: import_data
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_raw <- read_csv("data/relationship_preferences_data.csv")
```

### Check Imported Dataset

```{r}
#| label: check_imported_data
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

dim(study_raw)

names(study_raw)

head(study_raw)
```

*add snippet here describeing the above output*

### Create Analysis Dataset

```{r}
#| label: create_analysis_dataset
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false


  
study_vars <- c(
  
      "condition",  
      "age", "gender", "relationship",
  
      "IAS_1","IAS_2","IAS_3","IAS_4", "IAS_5","IAS_6",
      "IAS_7","IAS_8","IAS_9","IAS_10", 
      
      "AAS_1","AAS_2","AAS_3","AAS_4","AAS_5","AAS_6","AAS_7",
      "AAS_8","AAS_9","AAS_10","AAS_11","AAS_12", "AAS_13","AAS_14",
      "AAS_15","AAS_16","AAS_17","AAS_18",  
      
      "ETTR_1", "ETTR_2", "ETTR_3", "ETTR_4", "ETTR_5", "ETTR_6", 
      "ETTR_7", "ETTR_8", "ETTR_9", "ETTR_10", "ETTR_11", "ETTR_12", 
      "ETTR_13", "ETTR_14", "ETTR_15", "ETTR_16", "ETTR_17", "ETTR_18", 
      "ETTR_19"
)


study_data <- study_raw %>%
  select(all_of(study_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))
```

### Check Analysis Dataset

```{r}
#| label: check_analysis_dataset
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

length(study_vars)

dim(study_data)

names(study_data)
```

*add a snippet explaining above output*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Variable Preparation

*add snippet text here*

### Prepare Participant ID and Experimental Condition

```{r}
#| label: prepare_id_and_condition
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    id = row_number(),
    condition = factor(
      condition,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Low", "High")
    )
  )
```

*add snippet explaining output*

### Check Experimental Condition Coding

```{r}
#| label: check_condition_coding
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data %>%
  count(condition) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Condition", "n")
  )
```

*add snippet here*

### Check Prepared Analysis Dataset

```{r}
#| label: check_prepared_analysis_dataset
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

dim(study_data)

names(study_data)
```

*add snippet here about output above*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Reverse-Code Scale Items

*add snippet here about reverse coding the*

```{r}
#| label: reverse_code_items
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    IAS_3r = 8 - IAS_3,
    IAS_4r = 8 - IAS_4,
    IAS_5r = 8 - IAS_5,
    
    ETTR_2r  = 7 - ETTR_2,
    ETTR_5r  = 7 - ETTR_5,
    ETTR_7r  = 7 - ETTR_7,
    ETTR_10r = 7 - ETTR_10,
    ETTR_13r = 7 - ETTR_13,
    ETTR_16r = 7 - ETTR_16,
    ETTR_18r = 7 - ETTR_18,
    
    AAS_8r  = 6 - AAS_8,
    AAS_13r = 6 - AAS_13,
    AAS_17r = 6 - AAS_17,
    AAS_2r  = 6 - AAS_2,
    AAS_16r = 6 - AAS_16,
    AAS_18r = 6 - AAS_18,
    
    AAS_3r  = 6 - AAS_3,
    AAS_4r  = 6 - AAS_4,
    AAS_9r  = 6 - AAS_9,
    AAS_10r = 6 - AAS_10,
    AAS_11r = 6 - AAS_11,
    AAS_15r = 6 - AAS_15
  )
```

### Check Reverse Coding

```{r}
#| label: check_reverse_coding
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data %>%
  select(IAS_3,IAS_3r, ETTR_2, ETTR_2r, AAS_3, AAS_3r) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

*add snippet here about output above*

### Create Scale Scores

*add snippet here*

### ***Note: For this analysis workflow, the AAS items were reverse-scored so that all attachment scale scores point in a positive direction. Higher scores indicate more adaptive attachment-related outcomes. Specifically, higher total AAS scores indicate higher overall attachment security, higher closeness scores indicate greater comfort with closeness, higher dependence scores indicate greater comfort depending on others, and higher anxiety-security scores indicate lower attachment anxiety.***

```{r}
#| label: create_scale_scores
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Define IAS social attraction items
IAS_items <- c("IAS_1", "IAS_2", "IAS_3r", "IAS_4r", "IAS_5r", "IAS_6")

# Define revised AAS close, depend, and anxiety-security items
aas_close_items <- c("AAS_1", "AAS_6", "AAS_8r", "AAS_12", "AAS_13r", "AAS_17r")

aas_depend_items <- c("AAS_2r", "AAS_5", "AAS_7", "AAS_14", "AAS_16r", "AAS_18r")

aas_anxiety_items <- c("AAS_3r", "AAS_4r", "AAS_9r", "AAS_10r", "AAS_11r", "AAS_15r")

aas_total_items <- c(aas_close_items, aas_depend_items, aas_anxiety_items)

# Define ETRR relationship expectation items
ETTR_items <- c("ETTR_1", "ETTR_2r", "ETTR_3", "ETTR_4", "ETTR_5r", "ETTR_6", 
                "ETTR_7r", "ETTR_8", "ETTR_9", "ETTR_10r", "ETTR_11", "ETTR_12", 
                "ETTR_13r", "ETTR_14", "ETTR_15", "ETTR_16r", "ETTR_17", "ETTR_18r", "ETTR_19")

study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    IAS_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(IAS_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_close_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_close_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_depend_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_depend_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_anxiety_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_anxiety_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    aas_total_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(aas_total_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    ETTR_scale = rowMeans(across(all_of(ETTR_items)), na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

### Check Scale Scores

```{r}
#| label: check_scale_scores
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data %>%
  select(IAS_scale, aas_close_scale, aas_depend_scale, aas_anxiety_scale, aas_total_scale, ETTR_scale) %>%
  summary()

study_data %>%
  summarise(
    IAS_missing = mean(is.na(IAS_scale)),
    aas_close_missing = mean(is.na(aas_close_scale)),
    aas_depend_missing = mean(is.na(aas_depend_scale)),
    aas_anxiety_missing = mean(is.na(aas_anxiety_scale)),
    aas_total_missing = mean(is.na(aas_total_scale)),
    ETTR_missing = mean(is.na(ETTR_scale))
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

*add snippet here about output above*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Create Attachment Anxiety Groups

*add snippet here*

```{r}
#| label: create_attachment_groups
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Create median values for the attachment scale scores
aas_total_median <- median(study_data$aas_total_scale, na.rm = TRUE)
aas_close_median <- median(study_data$aas_close_scale, na.rm = TRUE)
aas_depend_median <- median(study_data$aas_depend_scale, na.rm = TRUE)
aas_anxiety_median <- median(study_data$aas_anxiety_scale, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create median-split attachment groups
study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    aas_total_group = if_else(aas_total_scale <= aas_total_median, "Lower attachment security", "Higher attachment security"),
    aas_total_group = factor(aas_total_group, levels = c("Lower attachment security", "Higher attachment security")),
    
    aas_close_group = if_else(aas_close_scale <= aas_close_median, "Lower closeness security", "Higher closeness security"),
    aas_close_group = factor(aas_close_group, levels = c("Lower closeness security", "Higher closeness security")),
    
    aas_depend_group = if_else(aas_depend_scale <= aas_depend_median, "Lower dependence security", "Higher dependence security"),
    aas_depend_group = factor(aas_depend_group, levels = c("Lower dependence security", "Higher dependence security")),
    
    aas_anxiety_group = if_else(aas_anxiety_scale <= aas_anxiety_median, "Lower anxiety security", "Higher anxiety security"),
    aas_anxiety_group = factor(aas_anxiety_group, levels = c("Lower anxiety security", "Higher anxiety security"))
  )

# Check final attachment group sizes
study_data %>%
  count(aas_total_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()

study_data %>%
  count(aas_close_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()

study_data %>%
  count(aas_depend_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()

study_data %>%
  count(aas_anxiety_group) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

*add snippet here about output above*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Check Scale Reliability

*add snippet here*

```{r}
#| label: reliability_checks
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Create item-only data frames for each scale
IAS_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(IAS_items))
aas_close_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_close_items))
aas_depend_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_depend_items))
aas_anxiety_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_anxiety_items))
aas_total_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(aas_total_items))
ETTR_item_df <- study_data %>% select(all_of(ETTR_items))

# Compute internal consistency estimates
IAS_alpha <- psych::alpha(IAS_item_df)
IAS_omega <- psych::omega(IAS_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_close_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_close_item_df)
aas_close_omega <- psych::omega(aas_close_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_depend_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_depend_item_df)
aas_depend_omega <- psych::omega(aas_depend_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_anxiety_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_anxiety_item_df)
aas_anxiety_omega <- psych::omega(aas_anxiety_item_df, plot = FALSE)

aas_total_alpha <- psych::alpha(aas_total_item_df)
aas_total_omega <- psych::omega(aas_total_item_df, plot = FALSE)

ETTR_alpha <- psych::alpha(ETTR_item_df)
ETTR_omega <- psych::omega(ETTR_item_df, plot = FALSE)

# Print only alpha and omega coefficients
tibble(
  scale = c("Social Attraction", "AAS Close Security", "AAS Depend Security", "AAS Anxiety Security", "AAS Total Security", "Relationship Expectation"),
  alpha = c(IAS_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_close_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_depend_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_anxiety_alpha$total$raw_alpha, aas_total_alpha$total$raw_alpha, ETTR_alpha$total$raw_alpha),
  omega = c(IAS_omega$omega.tot, aas_close_omega$omega.tot, aas_depend_omega$omega.tot, aas_anxiety_omega$omega.tot, aas_total_omega$omega.tot, ETTR_omega$omega.tot)
) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

*add snippet here about output above*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Descriptive Statistics

*add snippet here*

### Scale Score Descriptives

```{r}
#| label: scale_score_descriptives
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data %>%
  select(
    IAS_scale,
    aas_close_scale,
    aas_depend_scale,
    aas_anxiety_scale,
    aas_total_scale,
    ETTR_scale
  ) %>%
  psych::describe() %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)
```

*add snippet here about output above*

### Sample Composition by Age, Gender, and Relationship

```{r}
#| label: sample_composition_categorical_variables
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

study_data %>%
  count(age) %>%
  arrange(age) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Age", "n", "Percent"),
    caption = "Sample Composition by Age"
  )

study_data %>%
  count(gender) %>%
  arrange(gender) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Gender", "n", "Percent"),
    caption = "Sample Composition by Gender"
  )

study_data %>%
  count(relationship) %>%
  arrange(relationship) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Relationship", "n", "Percent"),
    caption = "Sample Composition by Year"
  )
```

*add snippet describing output*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Correlations Among the DVs

*add snippet here*

```{r}
#| label: correlation_test
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Correlations for the main scale scores
psych::corr.test(
  study_data %>% 
    select(
      IAS_scale,
      aas_close_scale,
      aas_depend_scale,
      aas_anxiety_scale,
      aas_total_scale,
      ETTR_scale
    )
)
```

## Primary Analyses

*Add snippet about what ANOVAS you are going to conduct.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Relationship Expectation Model 1: Total Attachment Security

```{r}
#| label: anova_relationship_expectation_total_attachment
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting relationship expectation scores
# DV: ETTR_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_total_group
a1_ETTR_total <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_total_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_ETTR_total
```

*Add snippet about output here.*

### Estimated Means for Relationship Expectation: Total Attachment Security

```{r}
#| label: estimated_means_relationship_expectation_total_attachment
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ condition)

emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ aas_total_group)

emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ condition * aas_total_group)
```

*Add snippet about estimated means here.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Relationship Expectation Model 2: Closeness Security

```{r}
#| label: anova_relationship_expectation_closeness
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting relationship expectation scores
# DV: ETTR_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_close_group
a1_ETTR_close <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_close_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_ETTR_close
```

*Add snippet about output here.*

### Estimated Means for Relationship Expectation: Closeness Security

```{r}
#| label: estimated_means_relationship_expectation_closeness
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

emmeans(a1_ETTR_close, ~ condition)

emmeans(a1_ETTR_close, ~ aas_close_group)

emmeans(a1_ETTR_close, ~ condition * aas_close_group)
```

*Add snippet about estimated means here.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Relationship Expectation Model 3: Dependence Security

```{r}
#| label: anova_relationship_expectation_dependence
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting relationship expectation scores
# DV: ETTR_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_depend_group
a1_ETTR_depend <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_depend_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_ETTR_depend
```

```{r}
#| label: estimated_means_relationship_expectation_dependence

```

```{r}
  id = "id",
  dv = "ETTR_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_anxiety_group"),

a1_ETTR_anxiety
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# 2x2 between-subjects ANOVA predicting social attraction scores
# DV: IAS_scale
# Factors: condition and aas_total_group
a1_IAS_total <- afex::aov_ez(
  id = "id",
  dv = "IAS_scale",
  between = c("condition", "aas_total_group"),
  data = study_data
)

# Print the ANOVA table
a1_IAS_total
```

## Figures

*Add snippet here.*

### Relationship Expectation Interaction Plot: Total Attachment Security

```{r}
#| label: interaction_plot_relationship_expectation_total_attachment
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

p_ETTR_total_bar <- ggplot(
  as.data.frame(emmeans(a1_ETTR_total, ~ condition * aas_total_group)),
  aes(
    x = condition,
    y = emmean,
    fill = aas_total_group
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.55,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Communication Condition",
    y = "Mean Relationship Expectation Score",
    fill = "Total Attachment Security"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Lower attachment security" = "#E76F51",  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
      "Higher attachment security" = "#2A9D8F"  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:6
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(1, 6)
  ) +
  theme_classic(
    base_size = 14
  )

p_ETTR_total_bar
```

*Add snippet about the above figure here.*

### Relationship Expectation Interaction Plot: Closeness Security

```{r}
#| label: interaction_plot_relationship_expectation_closeness
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

p_ETTR_close_bar <- ggplot(
  as.data.frame(emmeans(a1_ETTR_close, ~ condition * aas_close_group)),
  aes(
    x = condition,
    y = emmean,
    fill = aas_close_group
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.55,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Communication Condition",
    y = "Mean Relationship Expectation Score",
    fill = "Closeness Security"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Lower closeness security" = "#E76F51",  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
      "Higher closeness security" = "#2A9D8F"  # YOU CAN CHANGE THESE TWO HEX VALUES TO MATCH YOUR POSTER! 
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:6
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(1, 6)
  ) +
  theme_classic(
    base_size = 14
  )

p_ETTR_close_bar
```

*Add snippet about the above figure here.*

```{r}
  ) +
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Save FIgures 

```{r}
#| label: save_figures
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

if (!dir.exists("figures")) {
  dir.create("figures")
}

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/relationship_expectation_total_attachment_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_ETTR_total_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/relationship_expectation_closeness_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_ETTR_close_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/relationship_expectation_dependence_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_ETTR_depend_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/relationship_expectation_anxiety_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_ETTR_anxiety_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/social_attraction_total_attachment_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_IAS_total_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/social_attraction_closeness_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_IAS_close_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/social_attraction_dependence_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_IAS_depend_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/social_attraction_anxiety_security_interaction_plot.svg",
  plot = p_IAS_anxiety_bar,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Results Summary

*add a summary of your results with not statistics here.*

## Reproducibility Information

*Rephrase this language in your own words :)*

This section records the R version, operating system, and package versions used to render the report. Including this information helps another reader understand the software environment used to reproduce the analyses.

```{r}
#| label: session_info
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

sessionInfo()
```
